title: Stage Writing Basics
description: Dialogue-driven, actor-friendly text: subtext, beats, stage directions, silence.
summary_pt_br: Texto teatral: diálogo conduz, subtexto embaixo, beats, rubricas mínimas, silêncio é fala.

scenes.subtitle_en: Dialogue-driven, actor-friendly text: subtext, beats, stage directions, silence.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Texto teatral: diálogo conduz, subtexto embaixo, beats, rubricas mínimas, silêncio é fala.

scenes.text_en: Dialogue-driven, actor-friendly text: subtext, beats, stage directions, silence. Focus: stage writing basics. She says 'I'm fine' — the subtext is 'I'm furious'.
scenes.text_pt: Texto teatral: diálogo conduz, subtexto embaixo, beats, rubricas mínimas, silêncio é fala. Foco: stage writing basics. Ela diz 'tudo bem' — subtexto é 'furiosa'.

scenes.title: Stage Writing Basics
scenes.body_en: Stage craft: DIALOGUE must drive action. SUBTEXT lives underneath. BEATS separate moments. STAGE DIRECTIONS minimal but precise. SILENCE is a line.
scenes.body_pt: Texto teatral: diálogo conduz, subtexto embaixo, beats, rubricas mínimas, silêncio é fala.

examples.en: She says 'I'm fine' — the subtext is 'I'm furious'.
A beat tells the actor where to breathe.
examples.pt: Um beat diz onde o ator respira.

examples.en: Silence, in stage directions, is a line.
examples.pt: Silêncio, na rubrica, é uma fala.

exercise.question: Complete: "She says 'I'm fine' — the subtext is 'I'm furious'."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "She says 'I'm fine' — the subtext is 'I'm furious'."

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: A beat tells the actor where to breathe.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "A beat tells the actor where to breathe."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Um beat diz onde o ator respira.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Stage Writing Basics — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre stage writing basics. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Rafa, what's subtext in one sentence?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: What characters mean below what they say.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: As few as possible, precise every time.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes — it's often the loudest line.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How do you test the script?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Read with actors in a room.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Stage writing is written for ears, not eyes.

scenes.title: The table read
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe four stage-writing elements. Use at least three stage terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: At the table read, six actors sat around a script that had been rewritten nine times in three months.

paragraphs.speaker: Director
paragraphs.en: She says 'I'm fine' — but the subtext the audience must feel is 'I'm furious and about to leave'.

paragraphs.speaker: Rafa
paragraphs.en: A beat here lets the actor breathe, and a beat there tells the audience to breathe with her.

paragraphs.speaker: Actor
paragraphs.en: Stage directions are almost absent — precise when they appear, silent otherwise, trusting the actor.

paragraphs.speaker: Director
paragraphs.en: The silence before she answers — that line is louder than everything she says afterwards combined.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Stage writing, Rafa believed, is the one form of prose written for the ear rather than ever for the eye.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Stage Writing Basics". Dialogue-driven, actor-friendly text: subtext, beats, stage directions, silence. She says 'I'm fine' — the subtext is 'I'm furious'. A beat tells the actor where to breathe. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Stage Writing Basics". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
check.explanation: She says the title: "Stage Writing Basics".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Stage Writing Basics".

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
